title: pittosporum napaliense
intro: Pittosporum napaliense, also known as Hawaiian holly or Na'ena'e, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pittosporaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Pittosporum napaliense include wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Pittosporum napaliense is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for various health benefits.
